validation.name: appstate-mutations
notes: Outside-Warp active target resolution required focusing the Warp window with xdotool before commands using --tab active or --pane active.

Metadata/configuration mutation permission was required for tab rename/reset-name/color and pane rename/reset-name, so the Settings > Scripting metadata/configuration toggle was enabled during validation.
A stale discovery record caused ambiguous_instance during setup and was removed before final command captures.
Supplemental screenshots 101-108 were captured because the first pass screenshots did not consistently show the command/output text in xterm.

visual_inspection_blockers: pane rename: JSON success but no visible pane-title/header evidence in screenshot
pane reset-name: JSON success but no visible pane-title/header evidence in screenshot
blockers: Pane rename/reset visual proof blocked by lack of a clearly visible pane title/header in the captured Warp layout.

commands.context: outside-Warp invocation from external xterm targeting a real graphical Warp app

permission_state.settings_visible: Settings > Scripting showed Warp control outside Warp enabled and app-state mutations enabled.

commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Show Warp raised/focused while the external terminal transcript shows the JSON success result.
commands.expected_result: Warp app receives focus/raises without changing tab or pane content; JSON reports ok=true.
commands.actual_result: Returned ok=true with action app.focus and the Warp window was focused/visible in the staggered composition.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: Warp window focused/raised relative to the external terminal.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Warp window and xterm were both visible; after command Warp was foreground/focused. The supplemental screenshot includes command, JSON output, and exit code.

commands.context: outside-Warp invocation from external xterm; Warp tab strip visible

permission_state.settings_visible: Settings > Scripting showed Warp control outside Warp enabled and app-state mutations enabled.

commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Show the tab strip before and after so the new tab count/label is visible, with terminal JSON showing previous_count and count.
commands.expected_result: A new tab appears and becomes active; JSON reports created=true and count increases by one.
commands.actual_result: Initial pass returned previous_count=1 count=2. Supplemental rerun returned previous_count=3 count=4 and the tab strip visibly had an added active bash tab.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: Tab strip gains one tab and active tab changes to the newly created tab.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Before/after screenshots show the Warp tab strip changing; terminal output shows count increased by one. Supplemental screenshot includes visible tab strip plus JSON counts.
unexpected_ui_changes: Additional Settings tab was opened during permission setup before later commands.

commands.command: $WARPCTRL --output-format json tab rename --tab active "WarpCtrl Validation Tab"
commands.context: outside-Warp invocation from external xterm; active Warp tab visible

permission_state.settings_visible: Settings > Scripting showed Warp control outside Warp enabled and metadata/configuration mutations enabled after discovery that this command requires that category.

commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Keep the tab strip visible and show the active tab label changing to WarpCtrl Validation Tab.
commands.expected_result: Active tab label changes to WarpCtrl Validation Tab; JSON reports ok=true.
commands.actual_result: Returned ok=true. Supplemental screenshot visibly shows the active tab label as WarpCtrl Validation Tab while xterm shows the command output and exit code 0.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: Active tab label reads WarpCtrl Validation Tab.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Supplemental screenshot clearly shows the tab strip label WarpCtrl Validation Tab with the terminal command/output visible.

commands.context: outside-Warp invocation from external xterm; active Warp tab visible
commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Show the tab strip with the custom label before, then show the active tab reverting to the shell/default label.
commands.expected_result: Active tab label reverts from custom title to default title; JSON reports ok=true.
commands.actual_result: Returned ok=true and the active tab label reverted to bash in the supplemental screenshot.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: Previously custom active tab label no longer reads WarpCtrl Validation Tab and returns to bash/default.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Supplemental screenshot after the reset shows the active tab label as bash while xterm shows command/output/exit code.

commands.context: outside-Warp invocation from external xterm; active Warp tab visible
commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Show the tab strip before/after to prove either a magenta color indicator appears or, if the exact color is unsupported, that the structured error appears and no misleading tab color changed.
commands.expected_result: Because the prompt required exact #ff00ff, either visible magenta tab color if supported, or a structured error if unsupported and no misleading visual state change.
commands.actual_result: Returned ok=false invalid_params with message #ff00ff is not a supported tab color; exit code 1. No visible tab color change occurred.
commands.stdout: {"ok":false,"error":{"code":"invalid_params","message":"#ff00ff is not a supported tab color"}}

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: No misleading color change when exact requested color is unsupported.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Supplemental screenshot shows invalid_params output and the tab strip remains without a magenta indicator.
visual_inspection.ambiguity_or_blocker: #ff00ff is unsupported by the current parser/app palette; no substitute color was attempted per instructions.

commands.context: outside-Warp invocation from external xterm; active Warp tab visible
commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Show the active tab with no custom color before and after, because the prior exact color set failed.
commands.expected_result: Command succeeds and active tab has no custom color indicator; visually no-op because #ff00ff set failed.
commands.actual_result: Returned ok=true. Visual state remained default/no custom tab color.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: Active tab remains/returns to no custom color.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: No custom color indicator visible before or after; terminal transcript shows ok=true.
visual_inspection.ambiguity_or_blocker: This is visually a no-op because the preceding color-set command was rejected.

commands.command: $WARPCTRL --output-format json pane rename --pane active "WarpCtrl Validation Pane"
commands.context: outside-Warp invocation from external xterm; active terminal pane targeted in supplemental pass
commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Show a visible pane title/header changing to WarpCtrl Validation Pane while the terminal transcript shows ok=true.
commands.expected_result: Active pane title/header visibly changes to WarpCtrl Validation Pane; JSON reports ok=true.
commands.actual_result: Returned ok=true. Initial pass accidentally targeted the Settings pane; supplemental pass targeted terminal pane Pane Pane Terminal (3125). However the screenshot does not expose a distinct pane-title UI/header, so the visual effect is not clearly proven.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: Pane title/header visibly reads WarpCtrl Validation Pane.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Terminal transcript shows ok=true and pane_id identifies a terminal pane, but the captured Warp UI does not show a separate pane title/header that changed.
unexpected_ui_changes: Initial pass targeted Settings pane because Settings was active. Supplemental pass targeted terminal pane.
visual_inspection.ambiguity_or_blocker: Visible pane-title affordance was not present/legible in the captured layout, so JSON success alone is insufficient for visible-effect validation.

commands.blocker_reason: No clear visible pane title/header in screenshot to prove visual rename effect.

commands.context: outside-Warp invocation from external xterm; active terminal pane targeted in supplemental pass
commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Show a visible pane title/header reverting from WarpCtrl Validation Pane to its default title while the terminal transcript shows ok=true.
commands.expected_result: Active pane title/header visibly reverts to default; JSON reports ok=true.
commands.actual_result: Returned ok=true. Supplemental pass targeted terminal pane Pane Pane Terminal (3125), but no distinct pane-title UI/header was visible, so the reset effect is not visually proven.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: Pane title/header no longer reads WarpCtrl Validation Pane and returns to default.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Terminal transcript shows ok=true and pane_id identifies a terminal pane, but captured Warp UI does not expose a separate pane title/header before or after.
unexpected_ui_changes: Initial pass targeted Settings pane because Settings was active. Supplemental pass targeted terminal pane.
visual_inspection.ambiguity_or_blocker: Visible pane-title affordance was not present/legible in the captured layout, so JSON success alone is insufficient for visible-effect validation.

commands.blocker_reason: No clear visible pane title/header in screenshot to prove visual reset effect.
